objections.detail: The ticket's Evidence section lists 6 files as untracked that have already been committed by auto-bulk commits f431fa7 (2026-05-24T12:40:02Z) and 83c5720 (2026-05-24T12:45:04Z). Specifically, these files are TRACKED AND CLEAN (not dirty) at the time of BA-QA validation: docs/dev/completion-20260524-101700.md, docs/dev/context-20260524-101700.json, docs/dev/qa-report-20260524-101700.json, docs/dev/ticket-20260524-101700.md, docs/dev/ticket-20260524-122910.md, docs/dev/context-20260524-122910.json. Additionally, docs/dev/dev-report-20260524-101700.json is no longer in ' M' (tracked-modified) state. The actual current git status --porcelain output shows completely different dirty files: ' M commands/INDEX.md', ' M commands/commit.md', ' M docs/dev/INDEX.md', ' M docs/dev/ticket-20260524-122910.md', ' M hooks/INDEX.md', ' M hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py', '?? docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-123039.json', '?? docs/dev/context-20260524-123039.json', '?? docs/dev/style-inspector-report-20260524-101700.json'. The ticket's M1 staging list is therefore directing dev to stage files that are already committed (a no-op at best, an error at worst).
objections.evidence: git log --oneline shows f431fa7 committed completion-20260524-101700.md, context-20260524-101700.json, dev-report-20260524-101700.json, qa-report-20260524-101700.json, ticket-20260524-101700.md. Commit 83c5720 committed context-20260524-122910.json, ticket-20260524-122910.md. git status --porcelain at BA-QA time shows none of these as dirty.
objections.required_fix: BA must re-run git status --porcelain, replace the Evidence file list with the actual current dirty files, and update M1, the Technical Hints staging list, and all AC1 references to reflect the real files that remain uncommitted.

objections.title: AC3 asserts /close todo state is resolved but workflow file proves it is ACTIVE
objections.detail: AC3 instructs dev to record in the note: 'No /tmp/claude-workflow-* file found for session c216ded5-b7e1-4432-b79f-a6a2042971fe as of 2026-05-24 — /close todo state is resolved.' This is factually incorrect on two counts. First, the workflow file EXISTS in the repo at .claude/workflow-c216ded5-b7e1-4432-b79f-a6a2042971fe.json (not /tmp — BA looked in the wrong location). Second, the file content shows last_todos with Step 6 ('QA validates BA conclusions') at status: 'in_progress', meaning the /close workflow for this session is CURRENTLY RUNNING (this very BA-QA invocation is Step 6). The /close todo state is NOT resolved; it is in_progress. Dev would be writing a false statement as a permanent artifact if this AC is executed as-is.
objections.evidence: File .claude/workflow-c216ded5-b7e1-4432-b79f-a6a2042971fe.json exists. Content: last_todos contains step 6 'QA validates BA conclusions' with status: 'in_progress'. The /tmp path referenced in AC3 is the wrong location — workflow files live in .claude/, not /tmp/.
objections.required_fix: BA must (a) correct the file location from /tmp/claude-workflow-* to .claude/workflow-*, (b) reassess whether /close todo state is actually resolved at the time the note will be written (it cannot be confirmed resolved until /close completes), and (c) either remove AC3 or reframe it as a future-tense assertion to be verified by QA post-close rather than a static historical claim to be written verbatim into the note.

focus_checks.title: AC1 robustness against further auto-bulk commits
focus_checks.detail: The revised AC1 uses three interlocking guards: (1) pre_dev_head=$(git rev-parse HEAD) as a causal anchor — any commit satisfying the Task-id body-line check must be AFTER the HEAD at dev invocation; (2) full path-limited clean check — git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=normal -- docs/dev/ must return no output (empty), covering staged, modified, untracked, and deleted states; (3) exact body-line marker 'Task-id: 20260524-123039' in pre_dev_head..HEAD range, not a substring match that could match stale auto-bulk commits. The NOOP branch (b) is explicitly gated on an empty invocation snapshot and does not require a new commit — AC2 and AC3 still apply independently. Live git status at QA iteration-3 time shows docs/dev/ has 7 dirty entries (5 tracked-modified, 2 untracked), so branch (a) will apply when dev runs. AC1 is not gameable: cleaning the tree via restore/delete without a commit would pass the clean check but fail the commit-range check; producing a commit with the wrong body line would fail the exact-match check.

focus_checks.title: AC2 structural soundness
focus_checks.detail: AC2 checks five concrete string-verifiable elements in docs/dev/note-20260524-123039-root-bin-manual-commit.md: (1) exact path /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh; (2) repo root /root; (3) 'git add /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh' command; (4) 'git commit -m ...' with descriptive message; (5) three Gap descriptions from close-report-20260524-101700.md. OBS-2 from iteration 1 confirmed the source file exists and lines 54-101 contain the three Gap descriptions. All five elements are grep-verifiable. No structural issues.

Step 6 status: 'completed' (this QA invocation completed it). The /close stuck state was genuinely replaced by the /redev invocation. AC3's assertion is factually accurate — the note will record a true statement.

OBJ-1.resolution: AC1 rewrote to be list-agnostic: uses live git status at invocation time with NOOP branch. The Evidence file list in the ticket body is now explicitly labelled non-authoritative (Technical Hints: 'Example files QA found dirty in a prior check (not a hardcoded list)'). Dev is MANDATED to run live git status at invocation time. The pre_dev_head causal anchor prevents old auto-bulk commits from satisfying the Task-id check.

OBJ-2.resolution: Confirmed in iteration 2: workflow file command field corrected from /tmp path guess to .claude/ actual path; 'command': 'redev' verified. Re-confirmed in iteration 3.

OBJ-3.resolution: commands/ and hooks/ files added to scope in iteration 2. Those files are now clean (committed by b58b4bc). No residual dirty files in those directories at iteration-3 time.

OBJ-4.resolution: BA addressed by adding live-snapshot examples to Technical Hints ('Example files QA found dirty in a prior check (not a hardcoded list): docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-122947.json, docs/dev/close-report-20260521-090300.md'). The MANDATORY instruction for dev to run live git status at invocation time means AC1 pass is conditioned on the actual working-tree state, not a hardcoded list. Additional dirty files not in the example list (ba-qa-report-20260524-122910.json, ba-qa-report-20260524-122947.json, context-20260524-122947.json, ticket-20260524-122947.md visible in current git status) will be correctly captured by dev's live git status run and staged under docs/dev/ — the Technical Hints are explicitly non-exhaustive examples.

adversarial_questions_checked: Can dev pass AC1 by restoring files (clean tree, no new commit)? NO — clean-tree check alone does not satisfy AC1; the commit-range check (Task-id body line in pre_dev_head..HEAD) also requires a new commit with the exact body line.

Can a stale auto-bulk commit satisfy AC1's Task-id check? NO — the pre_dev_head anchor requires the commit to be AFTER dev's starting HEAD, and auto-bulk commits that ran before dev started are outside that range.

Is the NOOP branch exploitable (dev claims empty invocation snapshot when it was not)? DIFFICULT — dev records the invocation_snapshot variable; QA can re-run git status at the same HEAD (pre_dev_head) via git stash/show to verify. The NOOP claim is falsifiable.
Does AC2's content check cover all required elements? YES — five concrete string checks. Source file confirmed present.
Is AC3's workflow-file factual claim accurate? YES — live verification confirmed 'command': 'redev'.

Are there new untracked files that the BA spec silently omits? YES — ba-qa-report-20260524-122910.json, ba-qa-report-20260524-122947.json, context-20260524-122947.json, ticket-20260524-122947.md are currently dirty. However, these are CORRECTLY handled by the MANDATORY live-git-status instruction and the Technical Hints' non-exhaustive framing. The spec's AC1 requires the working tree to be clean post-commit, which means dev must stage ALL dirty docs/dev/ files visible at invocation time — including these. This is not a spec defect; it is the intended design.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 9 findings (4 labelled BLOCKER, 4 MAJOR, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY). After caller classification per Rule 3: 2 in_scope_real_bug (findings 1 and 2); 3 in_scope_minor (findings 3, 6, 8 — line range truncation); 1 minor inconsistency (finding 9); 3 out_of_scope (findings 4, 5 — audit manifest and per-path verification are defensive hardening beyond user requirement); 1 observation_only (finding 10). Finding 8 verified independently: close-report line 106 is the last line of the MemoryMax block; ticket Technical Hints say 'lines 55-101' — confirmed truncated.

findings.finding: BA-QA report top-level verdict is still 'fail'; iteration 3 pass is nested but top-level is stale
findings.rationale: Top-level verdict field must reflect final iteration verdict for orchestrator close-detection to work correctly.

findings.finding: AC1's 'clean all docs/dev/' sweep is over-broad: it would force dev to commit unrelated active-session artifacts (e.g. ticket-20260524-125300-B.md, ticket-20260524-125300-C.md visible in live git status) into this task's commit. User requirement is sessions 101700 and 122910, not all docs/dev/ files regardless of session.
findings.rationale: Contaminating this commit with unrelated session-125300 artifacts violates the user requirement scope. AC1 must either filter by session-pattern or mandate dev records an explicit exclusion list for any dirty docs/dev/ files not belonging to sessions 101700, 122910, or 123039.

findings.finding: M1 hardcodes '6 tracked-modified + 3 existing untracked' and Won't Have clause says 'Committing any file not explicitly listed' — both contradict the live-git-status-is-authoritative design.
findings.rationale: Internal inconsistency in ticket body. Not a blocking dispatch issue by itself (live-git-status instruction is the operative rule), but creates a false constraint that could cause dev to discard files not in the old list.

findings.finding: AC1 clean check only covers docs/dev/; if commands/ or hooks/ re-dirty before dev runs, AC1 passes while those are left uncommitted.
findings.rationale: At iteration-3 time commands/ and hooks/ are clean. The Technical Hints say 'any other subdirectories currently dirty' — this is instructional but not in the AC1 verification clause. Acceptable risk for this cycle given current clean state.

findings.finding: Technical Hints cite 'lines 55-101' of close-report-20260524-101700.md; MemoryMax section extends through line 106 (verified: lines 102-106 contain the '4G/3G/2G' replacement block).
findings.rationale: Dev copying only lines 55-101 would miss the actual MemoryMax target values (4G/3G/2G). Minor but real omission in AC2's source reference.

findings.finding: AC2 requires 'git add /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh'; Technical Hints says 'cd /root && git add bin/happy-session-recovery.sh'. Semantically equivalent but inconsistent.
findings.rationale: Both forms stage the same file. Minor inconsistency in documentation quality.

findings.finding: Require committed invocation-snapshot manifest for auditability of pre_dev_head.
findings.rationale: Defensive hardening beyond user requirement. The pre_dev_head causal anchor in AC1 is already a strong design. A committed manifest artifact is scope expansion.

findings.finding: Per-path git diff --name-status verification for each file in the invocation snapshot.
findings.rationale: Adjacent hardening. The clean-tree + Task-id body check is the accepted AC1 design across all BA/QA iterations.

findings.finding: AC3 note wording should clarify that 'command: redev' proves the workflow is not a close workflow, not that all todos are complete.
findings.rationale: No PROPOSED_FIX required per codex output — observation only. The AC3 assertion as written is factually accurate for the user's stated requirement (confirm /close stuck state is resolved).

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: CF-1: top-level verdict updated to 'fail' with new_blocking_objection note. CF-2: new OBJ-5 raised as blocking. CF-3/CF-8/CF-9: surfaced as minor for BA cleanup. CF-4/CF-5: rejected as out_of_scope. CF-10: logged.

new_findings_from_codex.title: AC1 'clean all docs/dev/' sweep would force-include unrelated active-session artifacts
new_findings_from_codex.detail: Live git status at iteration-3 time shows '?? docs/dev/ticket-20260524-125300-B.md' and '?? docs/dev/ticket-20260524-125300-C.md' — files from session 125300 which is NOT one of the sessions this task is supposed to clean up (101700, 122910, 123039). The AC1 'THEN' clause requires docs/dev/ to be fully clean after dev runs. The Technical Hints say dev must stage 'all untracked/modified files visible at invocation time under docs/dev/'. This means dev would be required to stage and commit session-125300 artifacts as part of task 20260524-123039, contaminating this commit's audit scope. The user requirement is 'commit remaining artifacts from sessions 20260524-101700 and 20260524-122910' — not 'commit all dirty docs/dev/ files regardless of session origin'.
new_findings_from_codex.required_fix: BA must either: (a) scope the AC1 clean-check and Technical Hints staging to session-pattern-filtered files only (e.g. files matching '20260524-101700|20260524-122910|20260524-123039' in their path, plus shared files like INDEX.md), OR (b) add an explicit Won't Have / exclude-list for session-125300 artifacts and update AC1 to allow docs/dev/ to still contain unrelated-session dirty files after the commit. Option (a) is cleaner.

summary.release_recommendation: reject — codex identified a new blocking defect: AC1's clean-all-docs/dev sweep would force-include active session-125300 artifacts (ticket-20260524-125300-B.md, -C.md) that are unrelated to this task's scope. BA must scope AC1 and Technical Hints to session-pattern-filtered files (101700|122910|123039 + INDEX.md). Additionally: ticket M1 hardcoded count and Won't Have clause contradict the live-git-status model (minor cleanup needed); Technical Hints line range 55-101 truncates the MemoryMax block which runs to line 106 (minor). After OBJ-5 is resolved, AC2 and AC3 remain structurally sound — core spec is close to approvable.
